source_book: NCERT Class 12 Ch.6 Electromagnetic Induction §6.10 AC Generator — table-of-contents reference only; teaching authored from first principles. The atomic idea: a coil of N turns and area A rotating steadily at angular speed omega in a uniform magnetic field B produces a sinusoidal (alternating) EMF by Faraday's law. FLUX-LINKAGE CONVENTION (crucial): Phi = N·B·A·cos(omega t) is the N-TURN flux LINKAGE with N already folded in, so the induced EMF is eps = -dPhi/dt = N·B·A·omega·sin(omega t) — do NOT multiply by N a second time in the EMF. The EMF tracks the RATE of change of flux, so it is a SINE while the flux is a COSINE: a 90-degree phase lag — the voltage peaks exactly where the flux is zero (coil edge-on, plane parallel to B), and is zero exactly where the flux is maximum (coil face-on, plane perpendicular to B). The peak EMF eps0 = N·B·A·omega has omega INSIDE it, so cranking faster raises BOTH the peak and the frequency f = omega/2pi. A perfectly steady omega still gives a reversing sinusoid — the changing GEOMETRY alone reverses the output every half turn, no acceleration needed. Two continuous slip rings carry that natural AC out unchanged through fixed brushes; a single split-ring commutator would flip the connection each half turn to give pulsating DC. MECHANISM (pass 3): a dedicated beat shows WHY the EMF exists — every charge in the two AXLE-PARALLEL sides of the moving coil feels F = qv x B ALONG the wire (the push dies at face-on, where v is parallel to B, and peaks at edge-on, where v cuts B — the geometric origin of the sine); the top and bottom sides are pushed ACROSS the wire and drive nothing. Does NOT cover DC-commutator depth (one-line contrast only — though the inset split ring now rotates with a brush-contact swap and a rectified |sin| trace on the graph), transformer action, rms/averaged EMF, or AC-circuit analysis (reactance/RLC — next chapter). Built instrument-native in the straightforward (non-Socratic) model at the tighter ~20s/beat pacing (founder 2026-07-04): six motion-distinct teaching beats + one open explore state (seven states), each state exposing only its own contextual controls (Rule 31). DEMO-COMPRESSED omega: the real grid is 50 Hz (omega ~ 314 rad/s); the demo omega is compressed to 0.5-3 rad/s for watchability. Every trig argument is omega*t in NATIVE RADIANS (no radians() wrap). The RELATIONS are exact. Direction of the AC is shown WITHOUT a 3D hand — via the eps-sine graph polarity plus an external-circuit current arrow that flips each half-turn synced to the sine's zero crossings.

real_world_anchor.primary: The 50-hertz electricity in every Indian home starts as a giant version of this exact machine. At the Bhakra Nangal and Tehri dams, falling water spins enormous coils inside powerful magnets; at a thermal plant, high-pressure steam does the spinning; on the wind farms of Tamil Nadu and Gujarat, the wind does it. Every one is a coil turning in a magnetic field, and the speed of that turning is what fixes the steady 50 cycles per second the whole grid runs on.
real_world_anchor.secondary: A bicycle dynamo lamp — press the little dynamo wheel against the moving tyre and the lamp lights the faster you pedal. It is the same rotating-coil-in-a-field, shrunk to fist size, and you can watch the brightness rise and fall with your own legs.

B.name: Uniform magnetic field the coil spins in

N.name: Number of turns on the coil

omega.name: Angular (crank) speed of the coil — demo-compressed for watchability

theta.name: Rotation phase omega t — angle between the coil's area-vector (normal) and B; the single live parameter that drives the coil AND both graph dots in lockstep
theta.derived: theta = (omega*t_state) mod 2pi [t_state = state clock, Rule 26, NOT a slider]

Phi.name: Flux linkage through the N-turn coil
Phi.derived: Phi = N*B*A*cos(omega*t_state) [= N x single-turn flux; starts at max, face-on; range spans max slider settings N=300, B=0.5, A=0.05]

emf.name: Instantaneous induced EMF (the AC output)
emf.derived: emf = -dPhi/dt = N*B*A*omega*sin(omega*t_state) [range spans max slider settings; graph auto-scales in the explore state]

emf0.name: Peak EMF eps0 — FIRST revealed at S4

f.name: Output frequency
f.derived: f = omega/(2pi) [grid scale: 50 Hz => omega~314 rad/s; demo omega compressed]

formulas.flux_linkage: Phi = N·B·A·cos(omega t) — flux linkage of the N-turn coil; maximum face-on (plane perpendicular to B, omega t=0), zero edge-on (plane parallel to B, omega t=90 deg)
formulas.faradays_law_generator: eps = -dPhi/dt = N·B·A·omega·sin(omega t) — the EMF is the RATE of change of flux, so it is a sine while Phi is a cosine (90 deg apart)
formulas.peak_emf: eps0 = N·B·A·omega — the amplitude of the sine; omega sits INSIDE it, so cranking faster raises the peak
formulas.frequency: f = omega/2pi — cranking faster also shortens the period; omega controls amplitude AND frequency at once
formulas.output_form: eps(t) = eps0·sin(omega t) — a clean alternating (sinusoidal) EMF even at perfectly constant omega
formulas.mechanism: F = qv x B on every charge in the two axle-parallel sides is ALONG the wire — the microscopic pump behind the EMF; it is zero face-on (v parallel to B) and maximum edge-on (v perpendicular to B), which is exactly why the output is a sine
formulas.slip_rings: two continuous slip rings + fixed brushes carry the coil's natural AC out unchanged; a single split-ring commutator would flip the connection each half-turn -> pulsating DC

constraints: eps = -dPhi/dt, so eps (sine) is 90 deg out of phase with Phi (cosine): the EMF peaks exactly where the flux is zero, and is zero exactly where the flux is maximum
The EMF follows the RATE of change of flux, not the flux value — max EMF at the coil's edge-on instant (flux zero, changing fastest), zero at the face-on instant (flux max, momentarily still)
eps0 = N·B·A·omega depends on omega, so faster rotation raises BOTH the peak EMF and the frequency f = omega/2pi
A perfectly steady angular speed omega still gives an alternating (reversing) sinusoidal EMF — no acceleration is needed; the changing geometry alone reverses the output every half turn

The driving force is F = qv x B on the charges in the two AXLE-PARALLEL sides only — along the wire there, across the wire on the top/bottom sides (no drive); the push scales as |sin(omega t)|: zero face-on, maximum edge-on
Two continuous slip rings deliver AC; a single split-ring commutator gives pulsating DC — they are NOT the same part
A generator converts mechanical work into electrical energy and creates no charge; stop cranking -> flux steady -> eps = 0 -> bulb dark
omega -> 0 is graceful: no rotation -> constant flux -> eps = 0, eps0 = 0; the slider floor omega_min=0.5 prevents any divide-by-zero in f or period

aha_moment.statement: The voltage peaks when the flux is zero — that's where flux changes fastest.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: At the face-on instant the flux-graph dot sits at its peak while the EMF-graph dot reads exactly zero; a quarter-turn later, edge-on, the flux dot crosses zero while the EMF dot hits its peak — the two graphs are 90 degrees apart.

STATE_1.title: The machine: a coil spinning in a field lights the grid

scene_composition.text: AC generator: a coil rotating in a uniform field B
slip rings + brushes carry the current out
stop cranking -> bulb dark: work in, electrical energy out (no charge created)

tts_sentences.text_en: This coil, spinning between two magnet poles, is an AC generator — the machine behind the fifty-cycle power in every Indian home.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह coil, दो magnet poles के बीच घूमता है, एक AC generator है — वह machine जो हर Indian घर में fifty-cycle power लाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఈ coil, రెండు magnet poles మధ్య తిరుగుతుంది, అది AC generator — ప్రతి Indian ఇంట్లో fifty-cycle power ను తీసుకువచ్చే machine.

tts_sentences.text_en: As it turns in the steady field B, it lights the bulb, and two slip rings carry the current out through fixed brushes.
tts_sentences.text_hi: जैसे ही यह steady field B में घूमता है, यह bulb को जलाता है, और दो slip rings current को fixed brushes के ज़रिए बाहर निकालते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇది steady field B లో తిరిగేటప్పుడు, bulb ను వెలిగిస్తుంది, మరియు రెండు slip rings current ను fixed brushes ద్వారా బయటకు తీసుకువెళతాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: The bulb pulses bright, then dim, twice each turn. Stop cranking and it dies — a generator converts mechanical work into electrical energy, it never makes charge.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Bulb हर दो turns में चमकता है, फिर धीमा हो जाता है। Crank करना बंद करें तो यह बंद हो जाता है — एक generator mechanical work को electrical energy में बदलता है, यह कभी charge नहीं बनाता।
tts_sentences.text_te: Bulb ప్రతి రెండు turns లో ప్రకాశిస్తుంది, తర్వాత మసకబారుతుంది. Crank చేయడం ఆపితే అది ఆగిపోతుంది — generator mechanical work ను electrical energy గా మారుస్తుంది, అది ఎప్పుడూ charge ను సృష్టించదు.

STATE_2.title: Flux linkage: Phi = NBA cos(omega t), max face-on, zero edge-on

scene_composition.text: Phi = NBA cos(omega t)
face-on (plane perpendicular B) -> flux MAX
edge-on (plane parallel B) -> flux ZERO

tts_sentences.text_en: Follow the flux. The yellow area-vector shows which way the coil faces; when it lines up with the field B, the most field threads the coil.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Flux का पीछा करें। Yellow area-vector दिखाता है कि coil किस तरफ़ है; जब यह field B के साथ लाइन में आता है, तो सबसे ज़्यादा field coil से गुज़रता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Flux ను అనుసరించండి. Yellow area-vector చూపిస్తుంది coil ఏ వైపు ఉందో; అది field B తో సరిపోలినప్పుడు, అత్యధిక field coil గుండా వెళుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Turn the coil edge-on and the flux falls to zero. The flux linkage is Phi equals N B A cosine omega t.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Coil को edge-on घुमाएँ और flux शून्य हो जाता है। Flux linkage है Phi equals N B A cosine omega t।
tts_sentences.text_te: Coil ను edge-on తిప్పండి మరియు flux సున్నా అవుతుంది. Flux linkage Phi equals N B A cosine omega t.

tts_sentences.text_en: The green cosine graph traces it live — peak face-on, zero edge-on — its dot moving in lockstep with the coil.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Green cosine graph इसे लाइव ट्रैस करता है — peak face-on, zero edge-on — इसका dot coil के साथ lockstep में चलता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Green cosine graph దీన్ని live గా ట్రాస్ చేస్తుంది — peak face-on, zero edge-on — దాని dot coil తో lockstep లో కదులుతుంది.

misconception_watch.belief: In a uniform field the flux through the coil is constant, or the flux is greatest when the coil is edge-on.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The area-vector sweeps relative to B: the flux-through-face and the cosine graph both pulse from a face-on maximum to an edge-on zero, even though B never changes.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Flux linkage Phi = NBA cos(omega t) depends on the coil's ORIENTATION — maximum face-on, zero edge-on.

STATE_3.title: Why an EMF? The field pushes the charges in the side wires

scene_composition.text: F = qv x B on every charge in the moving side wires
only the two sides along the axle are pushed ALONG the wire
face-on: v parallel B -> no push   |   edge-on: v cuts B -> max push

tts_sentences.text_en: But what actually pushes the charges? Each side wire sweeps through the field B, so every charge riding inside it feels the force F equals q v cross B.
tts_sentences.text_hi: लेकिन charges को असल में क्या धकेलता है? हर side wire field B से होकर गुज़रती है, इसलिए उसके अंदर मौजूद हर charge force F equals q v cross B महसूस करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: కానీ charges ని నిజంగా నెట్టేది ఏమిటి? ప్రతి side wire field B గుండా కదులుతుంది, కాబట్టి దాని లోపల ఉన్న ప్రతి charge force F equals q v cross B ను అనుభవిస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Only the two sides along the axle get pushed lengthwise — watch the purple force arrows pump the charge, one side up, the other side down.
tts_sentences.text_hi: सिर्फ़ axle के साथ वाली दो sides ही लंबाई में धकेली जाती हैं — purple force arrows को charge पंप करते देखिए, एक side ऊपर, दूसरी side नीचे।
tts_sentences.text_te: Axle వెంట ఉన్న రెండు sides మాత్రమే పొడవునా నెట్టబడతాయి — purple force arrows charge ను పంప్ చేయడం చూడండి, ఒక side పైకి, మరో side కిందికి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Face-on, the sides slide along the field and the push dies; edge-on, they cut straight across it and the push is strongest.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Face-on पर, sides field के साथ-साथ सरकती हैं और push ख़त्म हो जाता है; edge-on पर, वे उसे सीधा काटती हैं और push सबसे तेज़ होता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Face-on వద్ద, sides field వెంటే జారుతాయి మరియు push ఆగిపోతుంది; edge-on వద్ద, అవి దాన్ని నేరుగా కోస్తాయి మరియు push అత్యంత బలంగా ఉంటుంది.

STATE_4.title: The EMF peaks where the flux is zero — the 90 deg phase lag

scene_composition.text: eps = NBA omega sin(omega t),  eps0 = NBA omega
EMF max where flux = 0 (dPhi/dt biggest) -> 90 deg apart
steady spin still reverses every half turn -> AC

tts_sentences.text_en: Now add the EMF — the bright sine curve — and it does something surprising.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब EMF जोड़ें — चमकीली sine curve — और यह कुछ हैरान करने वाला करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు EMF ను జోడించండి — ప్రకాశవంతమైన sine curve — మరియు అది ఆశ్చర్యకరమైనది చేస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Face-on, the flux is at its peak, yet the voltage reads exactly zero. A quarter-turn later, edge-on, the flux is zero but the voltage peaks.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Face-on पर, flux अपने peak पर है, फिर भी voltage बिल्कुल zero पढ़ता है। एक quarter-turn बाद, edge-on पर, flux zero है लेकिन voltage peak पर है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Face-on వద్ద, flux దాని peak వద్ద ఉంది, అయినా voltage సరిగ్గా zero చదువుతుంది. ఒక quarter-turn తర్వాత, edge-on వద్ద, flux zero కానీ voltage peak వద్ద ఉంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: The EMF follows how fast the flux changes, not how big it is, so it peaks where the flux is zero — a ninety-degree phase lag.
tts_sentences.text_hi: EMF यह देखता है कि flux कितनी तेज़ी से बदलता है, न कि वह कितना बड़ा है, इसलिए यह उस जगह peak पर होता है जहाँ flux zero है — एक ninety-degree phase lag।
tts_sentences.text_te: EMF flux ఎంత వేగంగా మారుతుందో అనుసరిస్తుంది, అది ఎంత పెద్దదో కాదు, కాబట్టి అది flux zero ఉన్న చోట peak అవుతుంది — ninety-degree phase lag.

tts_sentences.text_en: And the coil turns at a steady speed, yet the output still reverses every half turn. Steady spinning alone makes AC; the peak is epsilon-zero equals N B A omega.
tts_sentences.text_hi: और coil एक steady speed पर घूमता है, फिर भी output हर half turn पर उलट जाता है। Steady spinning अकेले ही AC बनाती है; peak है epsilon-zero equals N B A omega।
tts_sentences.text_te: మరియు coil steady speed లో తిరుగుతుంది, అయినా output ప్రతి half turn లో తిరగబడుతుంది. Steady spinning ఒక్కటే AC ను సృష్టిస్తుంది; peak epsilon-zero equals N B A omega.

misconception_watch.belief: The EMF is maximum when the flux is maximum; and steady spinning gives DC, so you must accelerate the coil to make AC.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: At the flat, face-on instant the flux graph is at its peak but the EMF reads exactly zero; omega is held perfectly constant yet the sine reverses every half turn.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: EMF tracks how FAST the flux changes, not how big it is — so it peaks where flux is zero, and steady rotation alone gives a reversing sine.

STATE_5.title: Peak EMF: eps0 = NBA omega — omega raises peak AND frequency

scene_composition.text: eps0 = NBA omega
crank omega up -> taller sine AND more cycles
f = omega/2pi -> grid 50 Hz needs a fixed steady speed

tts_sentences.text_en: What makes the voltage bigger? Crank faster and the sine grows — taller AND more cycles in the same time.
tts_sentences.text_hi: क्या चीज़ voltage को बड़ा बनाती है? Crank को तेज़ घुमाइए और sine बढ़ता है — taller भी और उसी समय में ज़्यादा cycles भी।
tts_sentences.text_te: వోల్టేజ్ ని పెద్దదిగా చేసేది ఏమిటి? Crank ని వేగంగా తిప్పండి మరియు sine పెరుగుతుంది — taller కూడా మరియు అదే సమయంలో ఎక్కువ cycles కూడా.

tts_sentences.text_en: Omega sits inside the peak: epsilon-zero equals N B A omega. Adding turns N raises the peak too, but only omega also raises the frequency.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Omega peak के अंदर बैठता है: epsilon-zero equals N B A omega। Turns N जोड़ने से peak भी बढ़ता है, लेकिन सिर्फ omega ही frequency को भी बढ़ाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Omega peak లోపల ఉంటుంది: epsilon-zero equals N B A omega. Turns N ని జోడించడం వలన peak కూడా పెరుగుతుంది, కానీ omega మాత్రమే frequency ని కూడా పెంచుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Frequency f equals omega over two pi. To hold the grid at fifty cycles a second, the coil must spin at one fixed, steady speed.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Frequency f equals omega over two pi। Grid को fifty cycles a second पर रखने के लिए, coil को एक fixed, steady speed पर घूमना चाहिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: Frequency f equals omega over two pi. Grid ని fifty cycles a second వద్ద ఉంచడానికి, coil ఒక fixed, steady speed లో తిరగాలి.

STATE_6.title: Slip rings deliver AC; a split ring would give DC

scene_composition.text: two continuous rings -> AC   |   one split ring -> DC
outside current flips each half turn (at the sine's zero)
split ring swaps contacts -> one-way pulsating DC

tts_sentences.text_en: How does the current leave a spinning coil without twisting the wires? Two continuous slip rings press on fixed brushes, one for each coil end.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Spinning coil से wires को बिना मरोड़े current कैसे निकलता है? Two continuous slip rings fixed brushes पर दबाते हैं, प्रत्येक coil end के लिए एक।
tts_sentences.text_te: Spinning coil నుండి wires ని తిప్పకుండా current ఎలా బయటకు వస్తుంది? Two continuous slip rings fixed brushes పైన ఒత్తిడి చేస్తాయి, ప్రతి coil end కి ఒకటి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch the arrow in the outside wire flip every half turn, right as the sine crosses zero — that reversing flow is alternating current.
tts_sentences.text_hi: बाहरी wire में arrow को हर half turn पर पलटते देखिए, ठीक जब sine zero को पार करता है — वह reversing flow alternating current है।
tts_sentences.text_te: బయటి wire లో arrow ని ప్రతి half turn లో తిరగడం చూడండి, sine zero ని దాటినప్పుడు — ఆ reversing flow alternating current.

tts_sentences.text_en: A single split ring would swap the contacts each half turn, forcing the output one way: pulsating DC. Two rings give AC; one split ring gives DC.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक single split ring हर half turn पर contacts को बदल देगी, जिससे output एक ही दिशा में जाएगा: pulsating DC। Two rings AC देते हैं; एक split ring DC देता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక single split ring ప్రతి half turn లో contacts ని మారుస్తుంది, output ని ఒకే దిశలో నెట్టి: pulsating DC. Two rings AC ని ఇస్తాయి; ఒక split ring DC ని ఇస్తుంది.

misconception_watch.belief: Slip rings and a commutator are the same thing.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Two continuous rings keep each coil-end on its own brush so the output alternates (the arrow flips); a split-ring commutator swaps the connections each half turn to keep the output one-way.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Two full rings -> AC; one split ring -> DC.

STATE_7.title: Explore: crank the coil, set omega, N, B, A

scene_composition.text: Set omega, N, B, A — watch Phi, eps, eps0, the bulb, the current
faster or more turns -> bigger eps0   |   omega also sets f
eps = NBA omega sin(omega t) — clean AC, 90 deg behind the flux

tts_sentences.text_en: Now it is yours. Set the crank speed omega, the turns N, the field B, and the coil area A.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब यह आपका है। Crank speed omega, turns N, field B, और coil area A सेट कीजिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు ఇది మీది. Crank speed omega, turns N, field B, మరియు coil area A ని సెట్ చేయండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch every quantity update live — the flux cosine, the EMF sine, the peak epsilon-zero, the bulb, and the flipping current arrow.
tts_sentences.text_hi: हर quantity को live अपडेट होते देखिए — flux cosine, EMF sine, peak epsilon-zero, bulb, और पलटता current arrow।
tts_sentences.text_te: ప్రతి quantity ని live అప్డేట్ అవుతూ చూడండి — flux cosine, EMF sine, peak epsilon-zero, bulb, మరియు మారుతున్న current arrow.

tts_sentences.text_en: One idea ties it together: a coil turning steadily in a field gives epsilon equals N B A omega sine omega t — a clean alternating voltage, ninety degrees behind the flux.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक idea सबको जोड़ता है: एक coil जो field में steadily घूमती है, वह epsilon equals N B A omega sine omega t देती है — एक clean alternating voltage, जो flux से ninety degrees पीछे है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక idea అన్నింటినీ కలుపుతుంది: field లో steadily తిరిగే coil epsilon equals N B A omega sine omega t ను ఇస్తుంది — ఒక clean alternating voltage, flux కంటే ninety degrees వెనుక ఉంటుంది.

assessment.mastery_definition: A student has mastered the AC generator when they can (1) explain that it converts mechanical work into electrical energy and creates no charge, so stopping the crank kills the output; (2) state that the flux linkage Phi = NBA cos(omega t) is maximum when the coil is face-on (plane perpendicular to B) and zero when edge-on (plane parallel to B); (3) recognise the crux trap — because eps = -dPhi/dt, the EMF is MAXIMUM exactly where the flux is zero (coil edge-on), not zero, a 90-degree phase lag between the cosine flux and the sine EMF; (4) explain that a perfectly steady rotation speed still produces an alternating sinusoidal EMF, since the changing geometry reverses the flux every half turn (no acceleration needed); (5) use eps0 = NBA omega to see that faster rotation raises BOTH the peak EMF and the frequency f = omega/2pi at once; (6) distinguish two continuous slip rings (which deliver the coil's natural AC) from a single split-ring commutator (which flips the connection each half turn to give pulsating DC); and (7) identify the microscopic driver — F = qv x B acts ALONG the wire only in the two axle-parallel sides (across the wire in the top/bottom sides), with a push proportional to |sin(omega t)|: zero face-on, maximum edge-on.

questions.stem: A hydro dam spins the coil of an AC generator, lighting a bulb far away. Where does the electrical energy delivered to the bulb come from?

options.A: From the mechanical work done to keep the coil spinning — the generator converts that work into electrical energy; it does not create charge or energy
options.B: The generator manufactures fresh electric charge and pushes it out to the bulb
options.C: From the magnetic field, which is gradually used up and must be replaced
options.D: From the coil itself, which stores unlimited energy and releases it for free

distractor_misconceptions.B: believes a generator manufactures charge, rather than moving existing charges by converting mechanical work
distractor_misconceptions.C: treats the static magnetic field as a fuel that is consumed; the field only mediates — the mechanical work is the real source
distractor_misconceptions.D: imagines the coil is an infinite free-energy store, ignoring that you must keep doing work (stop cranking -> bulb dark)

questions.tested_idea: An AC generator converts mechanical work into electrical energy and creates no charge; stop cranking and the output dies.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Explain what happens to the bulb's brightness the moment you stop turning a generator's coil, and what that tells you about where the electrical energy comes from.

questions.stem: As a generator's coil rotates in the uniform field, at which position is the magnetic flux through the coil the GREATEST?

options.A: When the coil is face-on to the field — its plane perpendicular to B, so the field threads straight through it (Phi = NBA cos(omega t) is maximum at omega t = 0)
options.B: When the coil is edge-on to the field — its plane parallel to B
options.C: The flux is the same at every position, because B is uniform
options.D: When the coil is spinning fastest; the flux depends on the rotation speed

distractor_misconceptions.B: swaps the two extremes — edge-on (plane parallel to B) is where the flux is ZERO, not maximum
distractor_misconceptions.C: assumes uniform B means constant flux, forgetting the flux also depends on the coil's ORIENTATION (cos theta)
distractor_misconceptions.D: confuses the flux (a geometric quantity, NBA cos theta) with the EMF, which is what depends on the rotation speed

questions.tested_idea: Flux linkage Phi = NBA cos(omega t) is maximum when the coil is face-on (plane perpendicular to B) and zero when edge-on (plane parallel to B).
questions.parallel_form_stem: State the coil orientation for which the flux through a generator coil is zero, and justify it using Phi = NBA cos theta.

questions.stem: A generator coil is momentarily edge-on to the field — its plane parallel to B, so the flux through it is zero at that instant. What is the induced EMF at that same instant?

options.A: Maximum — the EMF tracks how FAST the flux is changing, and the flux changes fastest exactly as it passes through zero (eps = NBA omega sin(omega t) peaks at omega t = 90 deg)
options.B: Zero — no flux through the coil means no EMF
options.C: Maximum, but only because the coil happens to be moving fastest there, not because of the flux
options.D: Undefined — you cannot have an EMF when the flux is zero

distractor_misconceptions.B: the classic trap — equates zero flux with zero EMF, missing that eps depends on dPhi/dt, not on Phi itself
distractor_misconceptions.C: reaches the right answer for the wrong reason; omega is constant, so it is the RATE of flux change (steepest at Phi = 0), not any speed-up, that peaks the EMF
distractor_misconceptions.D: believes an EMF needs a nonzero flux to exist, confusing the flux value with its rate of change

questions.tested_idea: Because eps = -dPhi/dt, the EMF is MAXIMUM exactly where the flux is zero (coil edge-on), not zero — the 90-degree phase lag.
questions.parallel_form_stem: A rotating coil is at the instant its flux passes through zero. Explain why its induced EMF is at a maximum there, not zero.

questions.stem: A generator's coil is turned at a perfectly STEADY angular speed. What does the output EMF look like?

options.A: A smoothly alternating (sinusoidal) EMF that reverses direction every half turn — eps = eps0 sin(omega t) — even though omega never changes
options.B: A steady, constant EMF, because the rotation speed is constant
options.C: An EMF that keeps growing, because the coil keeps turning in the same direction
options.D: Zero, because a constant speed means nothing is changing

distractor_misconceptions.B: assumes a constant rotation speed gives a constant EMF, missing that the changing GEOMETRY (orientation) reverses the flux every half turn
distractor_misconceptions.C: thinks continued rotation accumulates EMF, rather than producing a bounded, reversing sine
distractor_misconceptions.D: believes constant omega means nothing changes, overlooking that the flux still oscillates as the coil turns

questions.tested_idea: A steady rotation speed still produces an alternating sinusoidal EMF — the changing orientation reverses the flux each half turn, no acceleration needed.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Explain how a coil turning at a perfectly constant speed can produce an EMF that alternates in direction.

questions.stem: You crank a generator's coil twice as fast (double omega). What happens to the output?

options.A: Both the peak EMF and the frequency increase — eps0 = NBA omega rises with omega, AND f = omega/2pi doubles, so the sine gets taller and more frequent at once
options.B: Only the frequency doubles; the peak voltage stays the same
options.C: Only the peak voltage doubles; the frequency stays the same
options.D: Neither changes; the rotation speed does not affect a generator's output

distractor_misconceptions.B: sees the frequency-speed link (f = omega/2pi) but misses that omega also sits INSIDE the amplitude eps0 = NBA omega
distractor_misconceptions.C: catches the amplitude dependence but forgets that omega also sets the frequency
distractor_misconceptions.D: believes the output is independent of rotation speed, contradicting eps0 = NBA omega

questions.tested_idea: eps0 = NBA omega, so faster rotation raises BOTH the peak EMF and the frequency f = omega/2pi at once.
questions.parallel_form_stem: State how the peak voltage and the frequency of a generator each change when its rotation speed is doubled, and justify using eps0 = NBA omega and f = omega/2pi.

questions.stem: Two generators are identical except for the contacts that carry current off the spinning coil: one uses two continuous slip rings, the other a single split-ring (commutator). How do their outputs differ?

options.A: The two slip rings deliver the coil's natural alternating current (AC); the split-ring commutator flips the connection every half turn, turning it into one-way pulsating direct current (DC)
options.B: They are the same part with different names, so both give identical AC
options.C: The slip rings give DC and the commutator gives AC — it is the other way around
options.D: The split ring gives a perfectly smooth constant DC, identical to a battery

distractor_misconceptions.B: treats slip rings and a commutator as interchangeable, missing that the split gap is what reverses the external connection each half turn
distractor_misconceptions.C: reverses the two roles — continuous rings preserve the AC, the split ring rectifies it to DC
distractor_misconceptions.D: overstates the commutator output as smooth battery-like DC, missing that it is PULSATING (a rectified sine, not constant)

questions.tested_idea: Two continuous slip rings deliver AC; a single split-ring commutator flips the connection each half turn to give pulsating DC — they are different parts.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Explain why replacing an AC generator's slip rings with a single split-ring commutator changes the output from AC to pulsating DC.

questions.stem: As the generator coil rotates, which parts of the coil actually drive the current around the circuit, and at which instant is that drive the strongest?

options.A: The two sides parallel to the rotation axis — each charge in them feels F = qv x B directed ALONG the wire; the push is strongest at the edge-on instant, when those sides cut straight across B
options.B: All four sides drive the current equally, because the whole coil is moving through the field
options.C: The drive is strongest at the face-on instant, because that is where the flux through the coil is maximum
options.D: No side feels any magnetic force until the external circuit is closed and a current already flows

distractor_misconceptions.B: misses that on the top and bottom sides the force qv x B points ACROSS the wire, so those sides push charge against the wall of the wire and drive nothing around the loop
distractor_misconceptions.C: ties the driving push to the flux VALUE instead of the motion geometry — face-on the sides slide parallel to B (v parallel to B, so F = 0); the push peaks edge-on, exactly where the flux is zero
distractor_misconceptions.D: reverses cause and effect — the qv x B push on charges inside the moving wire is what CREATES the EMF, present even open-circuit; closing the circuit merely lets the pushed charges circulate

questions.tested_idea: The EMF is driven microscopically by F = qv x B on the charges in the two axle-parallel sides — along the wire only there, proportional to |sin(omega t)|: zero face-on, maximum edge-on.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Explain why only the two sides of a generator coil parallel to its rotation axis contribute to the EMF, and why their contribution vanishes at the face-on instant.

STATE_1.label: The machine: coil spinning in a field lights the grid
STATE_1.caption: A coil rotates in the steady field B; slip rings + brushes light the bulb. Stop cranking -> flux steady -> bulb dark.

STATE_2.label: Flux linkage: Phi = NBA cos(omega t)
STATE_2.caption: The area-vector sweeps relative to B; the flux-through-face and the green cosine graph pulse from a face-on max to an edge-on zero. The yellow arc is theta = omega t.

STATE_3.label: Why an EMF? F = qv x B pushes the charges
STATE_3.caption: Each axle-parallel side sweeps through B: every charge in it feels F = qv x B ALONG the wire — zero face-on (v parallel to B), maximum edge-on (v cuts B). Top/bottom sides are pushed ACROSS the wire: no drive. Slowed to omega = 0.8 for watchability.
STATE_3.formula_overlay: F = qv x B — only the axle-parallel sides drive charge

STATE_4.label: EMF peaks where flux = 0 — the 90 deg phase lag
STATE_4.caption: Faded cosine flux + bright sine EMF, 90 deg apart: at flux-max the EMF is zero; a quarter-turn later flux is zero and the EMF peaks. eps0 = NBA omega.
STATE_4.formula_overlay: eps = NBA omega sin(omega t),  eps0 = NBA omega

STATE_5.label: Peak EMF: eps0 = NBA omega (raises peak AND frequency)
STATE_5.caption: Crank omega up -> the sine grows taller AND fits more cycles (fixed axis +/-4.0 V). More turns N -> taller only. f = omega/2pi.

STATE_6.label: Slip rings deliver AC; a split ring would give DC
STATE_6.caption: Two continuous rings + fixed brushes: the current beads reverse and the outside arrow flips each half turn (at the sine's zero) -> AC. The rotating split ring's brush dots swap instead -> the dashed grey |eps| trace: one-way pulsating DC.
STATE_6.formula_overlay: two rings -> AC   |   one split ring -> DC

STATE_7.label: Explore: crank the coil, set omega, N, B, A
STATE_7.caption: Set omega, N, B, A and watch Phi, eps, eps0, the bulb, and the flowing current re-derive live (graph auto-scaled). Until you touch a slider, omega sweeps by itself — grab it to take over.
